Built 2007 · $364/sqft finished · Central A/C
Wayne and Michelle — Solomon Dr just hit the market 3 days ago and you flagged it quickly — good instinct. The views from this elevated Prospect Estates location are genuinely spectacular, and the virtual tours give you an excellent feel before your planned drive-by. A few things worth knowing going in: this is a bi-level with most of the living space upstairs; the 5,999 total sqft includes a significant amount of unfinished space and a 4-car garage that’s 1,108 sqft alone; and the lot is 0.43 acres in a closer-knit neighborhood than Cherokee or Lakota. The listing title calls it a “Mountain Castle Home” and the views back that up. Walk the virtual tours below, then drive by and see how it sits on the street. — Zachary

✅ What Works Here: The panoramic views from the upper main level are among the best in Prospect Estates — you can see snow-capped RMNP peaks from the great room and primary bedroom. No HOA is a meaningful advantage at this price point. The 4-car heated garage with workshop is exceptional and genuinely rare in Estes Park. City water and sewer. Main level primary suite. Just listed — you’re seeing it early.
⚠️ Know Before You Drive By: The 0.43-acre lot in Prospect Estates means neighbors are visible — this is a neighborhood feel, not a secluded retreat. The listing’s total 5,999 sqft includes substantial unfinished space; finished living area is 3,644 sqft. No Seller’s Property Disclosure was available at time of this report — request one before proceeding past the drive-by. The listing agent requires direct office calls for showings: .

Understanding the Square Footage
5,999 total sqft — here’s exactly what that means
📌 Important Context: The headline 5,999 sqft is the total structure. The finished living space is 3,644 sqft. The difference is primarily the oversized 4-car garage (1,108 sqft) and additional storage/workshop space. This is not a negative — the garage is a genuine feature — but it’s important to understand when comparing to Cherokee Dr’s 3,052 fully finished sqft at $392/sqft. On a finished-space-only basis, Solomon is $364/sqft vs. Cherokee’s $392/sqft.

🚗 The Garage: A Genuine Asset
At 1,108 sqft with heat, this is not a standard garage. It has workshop space and storage areas built in. For collectors, outdoor gear enthusiasts, woodworkers, or anyone with multiple vehicles and recreational equipment, this is hard to find in Estes Park at any price. If this matters to you, it’s worth $50K–$100K+ to build from scratch.
🏠 Unfinished Potential
The approximately 1,247 sqft of unfinished space represents future expansion potential. In Estes Park, finishing basement or lower-level space typically runs $80–$120/sqft — a meaningful add if you ever want to grow the finished footprint further. Verify exact unfinished area and configuration in person.

Market Context & Comparison
How Solomon compares to Cherokee and other active Estes Park listings at this price tier
📌 Solomon vs. Cherokee — The Key Question: At $130K more than Cherokee Dr ($1,325K vs $1,195K), Solomon offers a larger primary bedroom (24’×19’ vs 16’×14’), a 4-car heated garage, no HOA, and arguably better panoramic views from a higher elevation. Cherokee offers more finished sq ft (3,052 fully finished vs 3,644 finished but with significant unfinished space), a larger lot with open space backing, and a 50-year roof certification. Different tradeoffs — worth walking both.
